name: rasa.module.leads
description: Portable sales-lead + contact pipeline (capture -> qualify -> won/lost), mountable into any domain or orchestrator; every lead attaches to a customer account handle.

rasa.note: Business-ops sales-pipeline module — one of six siblings (leads, schedule, contracts, invoices, field-log) built to a shared account-handle spine. Ships the portable pipeline spine (content/leads-rules.md): the Lead record (id, account, contact_name, company, source, stage, value, owner, next_action, next_action_date, created, notes), the new -> contacted -> qualified -> proposal -> won|lost lifecycle, and the ledger discipline (LEAD-NNN monotonic, absolute dates, never-delete). The ACCOUNT SPINE is the coherence key: every lead references its customer by a stable @acct-<slug> handle; this module never invents or mutates the account's own record — the canonical account hub is canon task SA-032 (engagement-hub primitive), not yet a first-class primitive, so records join across the six modules by shared handle today and refactor to the hub FK unchanged when SA-032 lands. The ONE genuinely per-project concern — the exact pipeline STAGES + entry/exit criteria, what makes a lead 'qualified', and the lead-source taxonomy — lives in the project-owned .claude/leads-gate.md seam (seeded skip-if-exists); /qualify hard-stops until the qualification bar is filled. Cross-module hand-offs are SOFT (present-if-mounted, never requires.elements[]): a won lead can hand off to rasa.module.contracts (a contract) and rasa.module.field-log (a kickoff visit). DEFERRED SKILLS: the /lead, /leads, and /qualify skills are the authoring build phase, gated per BUILD_PLAN.md M-1..M-3 and deliberately held until a real consumer declares the module in requires.elements[] (the module.tasks / module.notes extract-after-proof precedent). v0.1.0 ships the spine + spec + seam + ledger template only.
rasa.convergence: One of six business-ops module shells sharing the @acct-<slug> account-handle spine (leads, schedule, contracts, invoices, field-log). The shared handle is what makes them a coherent CRM-shaped set rather than six disconnected lists; all six refactor to the canonical account-hub FK (canon SA-032) with no change to their records when the hub primitive lands.

identity.subject: leads
identity.goal: bring leads into a project as an installable RasaOS module

element.description: Element-owned files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The leads-gate seam and the live ledger are NOT here — they are project-owned (see seed.files).

files.note: The portable sales-pipeline spine: the Lead record's canonical field set, the new -> contacted -> qualified -> proposal -> won|lost lifecycle, the shared @acct-<slug> account convention, the soft boundary against contracts/field-log, and the ledger conventions. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. References .claude/leads-gate.md for the per-project stages + qualification bar + source taxonomy; it hardcodes neither.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

The v0.1.0 specification + build plan (the Lead entity model, the pipeline lifecycle, the leads-gate seam, the account spine, and the deferred /lead /leads /qualify skills gated per extract-after-proof). Author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.
Skill scaffold (opt-in). The generic /sync + /promote below install (directory-mirror); other skills stay opt-in.
Generic /sync — smart-pull upstream Element changes via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).
Generic /promote — smart-push local changes back upstream via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. These belong to the consuming project after install and are never overwritten on Element upgrade — the project's leads-gate adapter seam and its live pipeline ledger.

files.note: The per-project adapter seam — the ONE thing that varies per business: the exact pipeline STAGES + entry/exit criteria, what makes a lead 'qualified', and the lead-source taxonomy. Project-owned; the project fills it. /qualify hard-stops until the qualification bar is set. Ships with honest defaults + commented examples; placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

The live pipeline board — one entry per lead, grouped by stage, joined to the customer via the @acct-<slug> handle. Terminal won/lost leads stay in the ledger (a lost lead is data). Project-owned; grows over the project's life. LEAD-NNN monotonic, absolute dates, never deleted.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} via bin/init.

The DEPLOYMENT identity layer (SA-025) — project-owned; what this project is, for whom, on what platform. bin/init generates the element layer (.claude/rasa-identity.md) from rasa.identity every install.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. The leads/ ledger ships as a seed template instead, so this stays empty.
